name: Ginger (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw ginger rhizome, Zingiber officinale)

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 169231 (total sugars 1.70 g) apportioned by rhizome free-sugar ratio in Ghasemzadeh 2018 PMC6270952 (sucrose 33.5 / glucose 17.4 / fructose 8.6 mg/g DW)
components.evidence: Free glucose is the second most abundant soluble sugar in ginger rhizome; value = USDA total sugars split by the measured sucrose:glucose:fructose mass ratio (56:29:15).

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Least abundant of the three free sugars; apportioned as above.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Dominant free sugar in fresh rhizome; sucrose hydrolyses on drying/storage so ground ginger shows a higher glucose+fructose fraction (variety/processing dependent).

components.name: starch
components.source: USDA FDC 169231 by difference (carbohydrate 17.77 - total sugars 1.70 - fiber 2.00)
components.evidence: Starch is the dominant carbohydrate/carbon reserve of ginger rhizome (~40-50% of dry matter); not measured directly by USDA but obtained by difference.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Total dietary fiber.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Onwuka/Otunola-type analyses: 4.55 mg/g DW oxalate (scialert ijbc.2007.38.46) ~= 0.10 g/100g fresh; up to 0.23 g/100g reported (Adom/AHM 2022)
components.evidence: Most abundant organic acid in ginger (HPLC: oxalic > malic > citric > fumaric). Reported total-oxalate range 0.10-0.23 g/100g fresh; midpoint used. Includes soluble + insoluble oxalate.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: HPLC identification in ginger rhizome (Sci Total/J Ethnopharmacol S0926669015303538; researchgate 228476601)
components.evidence: Confirmed present by HPLC (second-most-abundant organic acid after oxalic) but not reliably quantified per 100 g fresh; often lost/undetected in dried forms. Present, unquantified.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: HPLC identification (S0926669015303538); ~2.1% w/w reported in a dried ginger extract
components.evidence: Present in fresh rhizome by HPLC; only small amounts survive drying. No trustworthy fresh-weight figure, so amount left null.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Detected as a minor/trace organic acid; unquantified.

components.name: L-ascorbic acid (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Vitamin C / ascorbate.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Total protein; the amino acids below are the protein-derived profile (largely bound, small free pool).

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid in ginger protein.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Second most abundant amino acid.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: Most abundant essential amino acid.

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Least abundant sulfur amino acid.

components.name: folate (vitamin B9)
components.evidence: Total folate.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral in ginger.

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: Phosphate source.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Low-fat matrix; fatty acid breakdown below.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant polyunsaturated fatty acid.

components.name: saturated fatty acids (total)
components.evidence: Chiefly palmitic acid; ginger essential-oil medium-chain aldehydes/acids (decanal, nonanal) are aroma volatiles, not bulk lipids.

components.name: monounsaturated fatty acids (total)
components.evidence: Chiefly oleic acid (18:1 n-9).

components.name: water
components.evidence: Solvent basis of the medium.

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: Synthetic contraceptive estrogen; biologically impossible in a plant rhizome. FooDB predicted/contaminant artifact.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Bacterial-fermentation food preservative (Streptomyces product), not a ginger constituent; FooDB contaminant/misassignment.

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant element, not a growth nutrient.

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: Structural silica; not a microbial growth nutrient.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-essential ultratrace contaminant; not quantified as a ginger nutrient.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt ultratrace; plants supply no B12, so free Co2+ here is non-nutritive trace/contaminant noise.

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: Ultratrace, non-established plant/microbial macro-nutrient; drop as non-nutritive trace.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Predicted stereoisomer artifact duplicating L-isoleucine already in the profile.

remove.name: Albumin
remove.reason: Generic protein class, not a discrete metabolite; subsumed by total protein / amino-acid profile.

remove.name: Prolamine
remove.reason: Generic storage-protein class; subsumed by total protein / amino-acid profile.

remove.name: Retinol / Beta-Carotene / Vitamin A
remove.reason: USDA reports vitamin A = 0 IU and beta-carotene ~0 in raw ginger; effectively absent, drop from the quantitative medium (trace at most).

missing_added: Individual free sugars with amounts (sucrose 0.96, glucose 0.50, fructose 0.25 g/100g)
Starch (~14 g/100g, the dominant carbohydrate) by difference
Organic acids: oxalic (dominant, ~0.15 g/100g), malic, citric, fumaric (present, HPLC-confirmed)
Quantitative amino-acid profile (18 amino acids, USDA)
Linoleic and alpha-linolenic acid amounts; SFA/MUFA totals
keep_uncovered: Zingiberene-related sesquiterpenes (beta-Elemene, gamma-Selinene, beta-Eudesmol, alpha-Elemol, gamma-Muurolene, beta-Phellandrene)
Monoterpenes: Camphene, beta-Pinene, Limonene, 1,8-Cineole, Geraniol, Citronellol, Citronellal, Neral, alpha-Terpineol, (+)-Borneol, 1-Isopropyl-4-methylbenzene (p-cymene)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169231 (Ginger root, raw; SR Legacy NDB 11216) - proximates, sugars, amino acids, vitamins, minerals, fatty acids
Ghasemzadeh et al. 2018, 'Changes in Nutritional Metabolites of Young Ginger in Response to Elevated CO2', PMC6270952 - rhizome free-sugar (sucrose/glucose/fructose) ratios
Otunola/Onwuka 2007, 'Nutrient and Anti-nutrient Constituents of Ginger', Int. J. Biol. Chem. doi:10.3923/ijbc.2007.38.46 - oxalate and mineral data (dried basis)
Chemical & antioxidant parameters of dried forms of ginger rhizomes, Ind. Crops Prod. S0926669015303538 - HPLC identification of oxalic/malic/citric/fumaric organic acids
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for growth are STARCH (~14 g/100g) and the free sugars (sucrose > glucose > fructose, ~1.7 g/100g total); the previously-missing organic acids (oxalic dominant, plus malic/citric/fumaric) add a secondary acid pool. Individual free-sugar values were obtained by splitting the USDA-measured total sugars by literature rhizome ratios (variety- and processing-dependent, so medium confidence on the sugar split); all other quantities are direct USDA measurements. Organic-acid amounts other than oxalic are HPLC-confirmed but not reliably quantified per 100 g fresh, so left null.
